summary: Tested NotaryChain Ceremony Video Vault P4 additions (Dashboard link, Daily.co auto-ingest endpoints, anchored REC badge on FL Journal entries) and the FL Journal HttpOnly-cookie auth regression fix. 17/17 backend tests pass (1 transient 502 cold-start on first run, all pass on rerun). Frontend cookie-auth fix verified — FL Journal now loads under cookie auth and the REC badge renders correctly with /verify/recording/{hash} links. Dashboard ceremony-vault-btn is present but click navigation did not change URL in the smoke test (minor — may be timing or onClick navigate latency, worth main agent verifying interactively).

minor.issue: Intermittent 502 cold-start observed once on first test run after idle period; all endpoints respond normally on rerun (Cloudflare/uvicorn cold-start).

ui_bugs.issue: Button is present with correct data-testid and text 'Ceremony Vault — Anchor RON recordings on-chain', but a programmatic force-click in 2.5s window did NOT navigate to /ceremony-vault (URL stayed on /dashboard). Could be onClick using react-router navigate with deferred render, or click intercepted. Worth verifying interactively that a real user click navigates.

issues: Filter dates use browser-default `<input type=date>` (mm/dd/yyyy native picker) instead of the shadcn Calendar component — inconsistent with rest of app polish.

Console hydration warnings (`<span> cannot be a child of <select>/<option>`) caused by a visual-editor overlay injecting <span x-excluded='true'> into <select>/<option>. Likely benign in production builds but should be silenced or the wrapper excluded from `<select>` children to avoid noise.

action_items: Verify Dashboard ceremony-vault-btn click navigates to /ceremony-vault in a live browser (possible onClick/navigate issue or transient render delay).
Optional: replace native `<input type=date>` on FL Journal filters with the shadcn Calendar picker for visual consistency.
Optional: prevent the visual-editor `<span x-excluded>` wrapper from being injected as a child of `<select>`/`<option>` to avoid React hydration warnings on NotaryFLJournal.
critical_code_review_comments: ceremony_video_routes.py L267: hasattr(db, 'fl_notary_credentials') is always True on Motor DB objects (attribute access returns a collection proxy), so the guard is a no-op. The subsequent try/except already handles it cleanly — drop the dead hasattr line to avoid confusion.

ceremony_video_routes.py /daily-webhook is PUBLIC and triggers asyncio.create_task() with attacker-controlled room_name. Acceptable because ingest_daily_recording aborts when daily_service.is_configured is False, but once Daily is configured in production this becomes a low-cost DoS vector (anyone can spam recording.ready events). Suggest validating webhook signature (Daily's HMAC header) before scheduling work.

fl_journal_routes.py L50-69 cookie-first auth correctly mirrors auth_routes.get_current_user. Good. Same pattern should be audited across other route modules that use Request directly (search for `Authorization` header reads to ensure none are still header-only).
ceremony_video_routes.py _finalize_video does not delete the partial S3 object on failure — orphaned uploads will accrue cost over time. Consider cleanup on status=failed.

ingest_daily_recording downloads via tempfile and uploads via boto3 — for multi-GB recordings this puts the file fully on local disk (/tmp). Consider streaming with multipart upload to keep memory/disk usage bounded.

success_rate.backend: 100% (17/17 after rerun; 16/17 first run with one transient 502)
success_rate.frontend: 85% (cookie-auth fix and REC badge verified end-to-end; Dashboard button presence verified, navigation needs interactive recheck)

test_credentials: notarytest@test.com / Test123! (notary), demo@test.com / Demo123! (user), admin@notarychain.com / Admin123! (admin) — all worked.
seed_data_creation: Created one TEST cookie-auth journal entry (ceremony_id=TEST_cer_<random>) and one TEST freeform ceremony-video upload init (immediately aborted). Both via API as notarytest. No persistent test data left in S3.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend P4 endpoints (/by-references, /daily-webhook PUBLIC, /ingest-daily/{room_id}, relaxed /init, public /verify/{hash}) and FL Journal cookie-auth (GET/POST /api/fl/journal/entries via HttpOnly cookie only) are all verified. The single remaining UI concern is whether a real user click on Dashboard's ceremony-vault-btn actually navigates — automated force-click did not within 2.5s. Daily.co is intentionally NOT configured in preview, so ingest-daily can only be tested up to the 503 'not configured' boundary. Test file /app/backend/tests/test_ceremony_vault_p4_journal_cookie.py covers all routes and can be re-run via `REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL=https://trust-network-dev.preview.emergentagent.com python -m pytest`.
rca of the issue: FL Journal cookie-auth regression — RCA confirmed: routes/fl_journal_routes.py::_get_user (lines 50-69) now reads request.cookies['access_token'] first, then falls back to Authorization header. Reproduction (before fix): login → cookie set → call GET /api/fl/journal/entries WITHOUT Authorization header → 401 'Not authenticated'. Mitigation (applied): cookie-first lookup mirrors routes.auth_routes.get_current_user. Verified by automated tests test_login_sets_access_token_cookie, test_list_entries_with_cookie_only, test_create_entry_with_cookie_only, test_no_cookie_no_bearer_rejected, test_bearer_fallback_still_works — all pass. Recommendation: extract a shared `resolve_user(request)` helper in auth.py used by both auth_routes and any other Request-based route to avoid this regression class.
